name: Trinity Credit Services
description_short: Texas-based credit repair company serving TX and LA since 1996, offering personalized dispute programs for mortgage readiness, auto loans, and general credit improvement.
description_long:
Trinity Credit Services is a McKinney, Texas-based credit repair company that has been in operation since 1996, making it one of the longer-tenured independent credit repair firms in the Southwest. With over 20,000 clients served, the company has built its reputation on individualized repair programs tailored to specific financial goals — whether that is qualifying for a mortgage, securing an auto loan, or passing a job background check. Trinity is a member of the McKinney Chamber of Commerce, is FCRA certified, and is listed with Dun & Bradstreet, though it does not hold BBB accreditation and currently carries a "Not Rated" BBB status.

Trinity's service model centers on two primary offerings: Standard Credit Repair and Credit Ready. The Standard program involves disputing inaccurate, outdated, or unverifiable negative items — including late payments, tax liens, bankruptcies, foreclosures, charge-offs, and hard inquiries — directly with all three major credit bureaus: Equifax, Experian, and TransUnion. The Credit Ready package is a more tailored intervention designed for clients working toward a specific milestone, potentially combining disputes, debt settlement guidance, and credit education. The company sets an expectation of 30-45 days for bureau correspondence and designs programs to be completed within approximately six months. Clients receive a login and PIN to access an online portal where they can monitor case progress and access educational resources including webinars.

What distinguishes Trinity Credit Services from larger national competitors is its personalized, boutique-style approach. Rather than processing clients through automated systems, the company emphasizes direct staff contact and tailors each plan to the client's specific credit profile and goals. Trinity also offers a Client Buying Assistance program that connects post-repair clients with an affiliated network of lenders for home and auto purchases — a practical touch that extends value beyond credit repair alone. The company claims a 100% money-back guarantee, asserting that deleted items will not reappear and that score improvement is guaranteed, though the specific conditions and eligibility requirements for this refund policy are not publicly documented anywhere on the website.

The most significant drawback of Trinity Credit Services is its complete lack of pricing transparency. No fees — whether monthly, setup, or total program cost — are published on the website. Prospective clients must call for a quote, which makes comparison shopping nearly impossible. One Yelp reviewer reported paying $900 for services, suggesting pricing is not budget-tier. Geographic coverage is also highly restricted: Trinity serves only Texas and Louisiana, making it unavailable to the vast majority of US consumers. The absence of BBB accreditation, a published refund policy, credit monitoring, and a mobile app further limit its appeal relative to better-documented national alternatives.

pricing.guarantee_details: Claims a 100% money-back guarantee with assurance that deleted items will not reappear and that score improvement is guaranteed. Specific refund conditions and eligibility requirements are not publicly documented. All pricing figures are estimated based on reported customer experiences — Trinity does not publicly disclose fees.

tiers.name: Standard Credit Repair
features: Disputes with all three credit bureaus (Equifax, Experian, TransUnion)
Removal of late payments, charge-offs, and hard inquiries
Bankruptcy, foreclosure, and tax lien dispute assistance
Online portal with case tracking and PIN login

tiers.name: Credit Ready
features: Goal-specific program for mortgage, auto loan, or job/security clearance readiness
Client Buying Assistance — access to affiliated lender network post-repair
Direct staff contact for ongoing personalized guidance

pros: 30 years in business — one of the longest-tenured independent credit repair firms in the region
Individualized repair plans tailored to specific goals such as mortgage, auto loan, or employment
Positive staff responsiveness and personalized service noted across multiple review platforms
Client Buying Assistance program connects post-repair clients with an affiliated lender network
Free initial credit report evaluation with no obligation to enroll
Online portal with case status tracking accessible via login and PIN
cons: Pricing is completely opaque — no fees published anywhere, must call for a quote
Serves Texas and Louisiana only — unavailable to the vast majority of US consumers
Not BBB accredited and currently holds a Not Rated status due to insufficient information
Money-back guarantee terms and eligibility conditions are not publicly documented
No credit monitoring, identity theft protection, or mobile app offered
best_for: Texas or Louisiana residents preparing for a mortgage or auto loan
Consumers who prefer a personalized, boutique credit repair experience over automated platforms
Clients with multiple types of negative items across all three bureaus
People working toward a specific financial milestone within a 6-month window
diagnosis: Best for TX or LA residents wanting a personalized, goal-oriented credit repair program with direct staff access. Main caveat: pricing is completely opaque and geographic coverage is extremely limited to two states.
services: Free credit report evaluation and analysis
Credit disputes with Equifax, Experian, and TransUnion
Removal of late payments, charge-offs, and hard inquiries
Bankruptcy, foreclosure, and tax lien dispute assistance
Credit Ready program for mortgage, auto loan, and job or security clearance readiness
Client Buying Assistance with affiliated home and auto lender network
Online portal with case status tracking
Credit education webinars and educational resources
Personalized repair plans with direct staff contact
typical_results_timeline: Bureau correspondence typically initiated within 30-45 days of program start. Trinity designs programs to be completed within approximately 6 months, though timelines vary based on the number and complexity of negative items on your reports.
